procurement_rules.label: High risk perishable protein
procurement_rules.recommendation: Accelerate procurement this week before further price increases. Prioritize frozen formats over fresh to extend shelf life.
procurement_rules.current_state: Perishable protein supply is under active pressure with prices already moving.
procurement_rules.trajectory: Conditions are likely to tighten further before stabilizing. Acting now locks in current pricing.
procurement_rules.rationale: Perishable proteins at high risk signal imminent price spikes or supply contraction. Early procurement locks in current pricing.
procurement_rules.procurement_note: Frozen chicken and pork offer best shelf life extension during perishable protein disruptions.

procurement_rules.label: Medium risk perishable protein
procurement_rules.recommendation: Consider buying 1-2 weeks ahead of normal cycle. Begin identifying shelf-stable protein alternatives now.
procurement_rules.current_state: Early pressure signals detected on perishable proteins. Prices have not moved significantly yet.
procurement_rules.trajectory: Risk is emerging and may accelerate. Preparation now provides flexibility before conditions worsen.
procurement_rules.rationale: Medium risk on perishable proteins indicates emerging pressure. Early awareness allows procurement flexibility.
procurement_rules.procurement_note: Dried beans and lentils are the most cost-effective shelf-stable protein fallback to begin sourcing now.

procurement_rules.label: High risk imported produce
procurement_rules.recommendation: Reduce reliance on affected imported produce immediately. Shift procurement toward domestic seasonal alternatives and canned equivalents.
procurement_rules.current_state: Imported produce supply chains are actively disrupted with availability already tightening.
procurement_rules.trajectory: Disruptions are likely to persist or worsen until underlying shipping or trade conditions resolve.
procurement_rules.rationale: Imported produce is highly sensitive to shipping and border disruptions. Domestic and canned alternatives offer supply chain stability.
procurement_rules.procurement_note: Potatoes and onions are the most cost-stable domestic produce alternatives with longest shelf life.

procurement_rules.label: High risk staple grains
procurement_rules.recommendation: Prioritize bulk procurement of at-risk staple grains immediately. Diversify across grain types to avoid single-grain dependence.
procurement_rules.current_state: Staple grain supply is under active pressure from trade, weather, or conflict signals.
procurement_rules.trajectory: Grain prices typically lag supply shocks by 2-4 weeks. The window to buy ahead at current prices is narrowing.
procurement_rules.rationale: Staple grains at high risk affect the broadest range of client meals. Bulk buying at current prices protects food bank budgets.
procurement_rules.procurement_note: Oats offer the best cost-per-calorie ratio among domestic grain alternatives and are predominantly domestically sourced.

procurement_rules.label: Medium risk staple grains
procurement_rules.recommendation: Increase standard order quantities by 20-30% this cycle. Prioritize shelf-stable formats with longest available expiry.
procurement_rules.current_state: Early signals suggest grain supply may come under pressure in coming weeks.
procurement_rules.trajectory: Conditions are emerging. Acting modestly now avoids larger cost exposure if signals strengthen.
procurement_rules.rationale: Medium grain risk signals emerging pressure on core meal staples. Modest procurement increase now avoids larger cost exposure later.
procurement_rules.procurement_note: Prioritize pasta and oats over white rice when diversifying — both are domestically sourced and less exposed to export ban risk.

procurement_rules.label: High risk shelf-stable protein
procurement_rules.recommendation: Stock up on shelf-stable proteins immediately. These are critical fallback items when perishable proteins are disrupted.
procurement_rules.current_state: Shelf-stable protein supply is under active pressure. Demand is rising as perishable protein alternatives are sought.
procurement_rules.trajectory: Demand for shelf-stable proteins surges during perishable disruptions. Supply tightens quickly. Act before demand spike hits.
procurement_rules.rationale: Shelf-stable proteins serve as the primary protein backstop during perishable supply disruptions.
procurement_rules.procurement_note: Dried beans offer best cost-per-gram-of-protein ratio. Peanut butter is calorie-dense and requires no refrigeration.

procurement_rules.label: High risk cooking oils
procurement_rules.recommendation: Shift procurement entirely to domestic vegetable oil. Avoid olive oil procurement until risk subsides.
procurement_rules.current_state: Cooking oil supply is under active pressure. Olive oil prices are already elevated due to import dependency.
procurement_rules.trajectory: Olive oil prices are likely to climb further as Mediterranean supply constraints persist. Shift now before vegetable oil demand also spikes.
procurement_rules.rationale: Olive oil is entirely import-dependent. Domestic vegetable oil provides equivalent cooking functionality at significantly lower cost and risk.
procurement_rules.procurement_note: Bulk vegetable oil formats offer significant per-unit savings for food bank kitchen programs.

procurement_rules.label: High risk dairy
procurement_rules.recommendation: Prioritize shelf-stable dairy formats. Reduce fresh dairy order volumes temporarily.
procurement_rules.current_state: Fresh dairy supply is under active pressure with cold chain and herd health signals active.
procurement_rules.trajectory: Fresh dairy disruptions tend to persist for weeks once animal disease or fuel cost signals are active. Shift to shelf-stable formats now.
procurement_rules.rationale: Fresh dairy at high risk creates spoilage and budget risk simultaneously. Shelf-stable formats reduce both exposures.
procurement_rules.procurement_note: Powdered milk and UHT milk are the most practical shelf-stable dairy formats for food bank distribution programs.

procurement_rules.label: Contamination risk — any food
procurement_rules.recommendation: Pause procurement of flagged items immediately. Switch to safe alternatives until contamination risk is cleared.
procurement_rules.current_state: Active contamination or recall signal detected on one or more foods.
procurement_rules.trajectory: Contamination events can expand rapidly. Do not wait for confirmation — pause procurement now and source alternatives.
procurement_rules.rationale: Contamination events require immediate procurement pause regardless of risk score. Client safety is non-negotiable.
procurement_rules.procurement_note: Canned equivalents and shelf-stable proteins are the safest immediate fallback during fresh produce contamination events.

substitute_categories.label: Perishable protein → shelf-stable protein
substitute_categories.current_state: Perishable protein supply is actively disrupted.
substitute_categories.trajectory: Shelf-stable protein demand will rise as perishable supply tightens. Source now before demand spike.
substitute_categories.rationale: Shelf-stable proteins offer equivalent nutrition with zero spoilage risk and stable supply chains.
substitute_categories.procurement_note: Dried beans and lentils offer the best cost-per-gram-of-protein ratio for food bank budgets.

substitute_categories.label: Imported produce → domestic produce
substitute_categories.current_state: Imported produce supply chains are actively disrupted.
substitute_categories.trajectory: Domestic produce availability will tighten as demand shifts. Move procurement before domestic alternatives also come under pressure.
substitute_categories.rationale: Domestic produce avoids import and shipping disruption exposure entirely.
substitute_categories.procurement_note: Potatoes and onions offer exceptional shelf life and cost stability as produce alternatives.

substitute_categories.label: Fresh produce → canned equivalents
substitute_categories.current_state: Fresh produce showing early pressure signals.
substitute_categories.trajectory: Fresh produce disruptions typically worsen before resolving. Canned alternatives provide stable fallback.
substitute_categories.rationale: Canned produce eliminates spoilage risk and is predominantly domestically sourced.
substitute_categories.procurement_note: Canned tomatoes are one of the most versatile and cost-effective pantry staples for food bank meal programs.

substitute_categories.label: Import-dependent grain → domestic grain
substitute_categories.current_state: White rice supply is under active trade or shipping pressure.
substitute_categories.trajectory: Rice export restrictions from major producers can persist for months. Diversify grain procurement now.
substitute_categories.rationale: Oats and pasta are predominantly domestically sourced and provide equivalent caloric value.
substitute_categories.procurement_note: Oats offer the best cost-per-calorie ratio among domestic grain alternatives.

substitute_categories.label: Imported oil → domestic oil
substitute_categories.current_state: Olive oil import pressure is emerging.
substitute_categories.trajectory: Mediterranean supply constraints are structural and unlikely to resolve quickly. Shift now while vegetable oil is stable.
substitute_categories.rationale: Domestic vegetable oil is functionally equivalent for cooking at a fraction of the cost.
substitute_categories.procurement_note: Vegetable oil in bulk formats offers significant savings for food bank kitchen programs.

substitute_categories.label: Fresh dairy → shelf-stable alternatives
substitute_categories.current_state: Fresh dairy supply is actively disrupted.
substitute_categories.trajectory: Dairy disruptions driven by animal disease or fuel costs tend to persist. Shift to shelf-stable formats before fresh supply tightens further.
substitute_categories.rationale: When fresh dairy is at high risk, shelf-stable proteins provide nutritional fallback without spoilage exposure.
substitute_categories.procurement_note: Peanut butter is calorie-dense and requires no refrigeration, making it ideal for food bank distribution.
